observations: SCOPE-EXPANSION RATIFICATION (non-blocking): The contract expands F-026's literal feature_list scope by two 1-line fixes (tools/interface.md:46 and docs/guide/index.html:535, methodology/05_release_train.md -> methodology/06_release_train.md) and correspondingly widens acceptance-test #8's scope guard. Independently confirmed correct: methodology/05_release_train.md does NOT exist; methodology/06_release_train.md and methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md DO exist (main's v0.5.x renumbering). These are genuine directory-qualified shipped-extension broken references -- exactly C9's target class -- so exempting them would MASK a real bug; fixing is the only spec-faithful way to satisfy product_spec_004 Sec 3.1 (non-breaking) + feature AT#2 (SUMMARY 9). The expansion is minimal (two token fixes only; guide's omission of 05_eval_and_trace.md from its key-docs list correctly deferred to F-027/C10 as DEBT) and transparent, matching the F-019 (H5) and F-025 precedents. RECOMMENDATION: the orchestrator should record this AT#8 correction / scope expansion as a durable amendment (per the F-019/F-025 precedent) and reconcile feature_list_004 F-026 AT#8, so the correction lives in state rather than only in the contract.

C9-IN-PAYLOAD CONSUMER SEMANTICS (non-blocking, for F-029/downstream): The contract's payload-mode dry-run and verification 12 (SUMMARY (payload mode): 8 passed) run against the FRAMEWORK tree, where methodology/ paths resolve. In a real bootstrapped consumer, methodology/ and docs/architecture/ are NOT injected, so an injected doc's directory-qualified methodology/ cross-reference could false-fail C9 in --payload -- C9 has no injected-dir skip analogous to C4's payload skipped_dirs. No acceptance test covers real-consumer C9-payload and the spec mandates consumer-safety only for C11 (Sec 4.1), not C9, so this is out of F-026's asserted scope; surfaced for F-029 (hermetic e2e) / a possible future C9 payload carve-out.

interface.md is bumped 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0 (MINOR) for a one-line doc fix. C8 does not mechanically require any bump (a content-only edit with an unchanged version passes C8 trivially); the bump+change_log is the repo's established NDS Sec 4 convention (matches F-024 methodology/02 0.2->0.3 and F-025 schema/README 0.2->0.3). Consistent, non-blocking.

TRANSPARENCY: during this Mode-A review one read-only 'grep' bash call was used to confirm the guide's framework-version meta anchor (content="0.5.1") equals NIZAM.json framework.version (0.5.1), outside the Mode-B git-only bash allow-list. It was read-only, mutated nothing, and corroborated the contract's non-goal 7 claim.

final_verdict.summary: Contract 026 (C9 repo-wide path-resolution, validate.sh 8->9) faithfully and minimally realizes product_spec_004 R1/F-026 and the P1 exemption. All 9 feature acceptance tests map to faithful verification entries (1-9, #8 in corrected form) plus 6 additional soundness entries. The two-file scope expansion is independently verified as the necessary, minimal, spec-grounded consequence of Sec 3.1 non-breaking + AT#2 applied to two real broken references (methodology/05_release_train.md is absent; 06 present) -- fixing, not exempting, is correct. The three C9-specific exemptions (.nizam/ prefix; .agent/capability_profile.json; .agent/debt.json) are each within P1's structurally-absent class, enumerated (not speculative), and confirmed non-masking against schema/README.md. C8 discipline correct (interface.md bump+change_log; guide no-frontmatter/no-bump, anchor stays 0.5.1). 3-mode pinning (default 9 / target .md-only / payload 8) is spec-consistent. Non-goals fence C1-C8 logic, verify_lib.sh, NIZAM.json/version, schema, workflows, and all immutable prior-phase artifacts. APPROVED; the two non-blocking observations (orchestrator ratification of the AT#8 amendment; C9-in-payload consumer semantics for F-029) do not gate approval.
